pattern: Greedily pairing the highest available digits builds the strongest half, with care taken so the leading pair is never zero and a lone middle digit fills the center.
hints: A palindrome is decided by its first half plus an optional single middle digit, so think about how to make that half as large as possible.
Count each digit; pairs of equal digits can mirror, and the largest leftover single digit can sit in the very center.

Build the half from digit 9 down to 0 using as many pairs as available, but never let a leading zero start the number; then place the biggest unused single digit in the middle and mirror the half. Handle the all-zero case as a single '0'.

edge_inputs.description: mixed digits with a clear best palindrome
all zeros collapse to a single zero
zeros can be interior but not leading
many pairs but lone large middle

stress.description: long random digit string with many repeats and zeros
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('0001234567899') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
